rules.name: belgian_nrn
rules.description: Belgian Numero de Registre National / Rijksregisternummer (YY.MM.DD-NNN.CC format)

rules.name: iban
rules.description: International Bank Account Number (2-letter country code + 2 check digits + up to 30 alphanumeric BBAN)

rules.name: eu_phone
rules.description: European phone number with country code (+32, +33, +49, +31, etc.) in common formats

rules.name: international_phone
rules.description: International phone number catch-all (E.164-like: + followed by 7-15 digits with optional separators)

rules.name: us_ssn
rules.description: United States Social Security Number (NNN-NN-NNNN format)

rules.name: uk_national_insurance
rules.description: United Kingdom National Insurance Number (AA NNNNNN A or AA-NN-NN-NN-A format)

rules.name: belgian_bce
rules.description: Belgian BCE/KBO enterprise number (0NNN.NNN.NNN or 1NNN.NNN.NNN format)

rules.name: be_id_card_number
rules.description: Belgian eID / residence-permit card number — 12 digits in 3-3-3-3 or 4-4-4 groupings, or run-together (covers eID and Carte de séjour/Verblijfskaart formats). Heuristic — also matches some long generic digit runs; if false-positive rate is high in practice, tighten with a discriminator on context ("carte" / "kaart" / "eID")

rules.name: long_digit_run
rules.description: Catch-all for ≥8-digit numeric runs that PR-CI guards against on path source bodies per §6.12.8 (see schemas.md). Flagged as indirect_identifier (advisory; not auto-reject) because legitimate runs occasionally appear in source URLs or reference numbers; the caller's LLM contextual pass disambiguates.

checksum_algorithms.modulo_97_belgian_nrn: Validate 11-digit NRN by verifying that the last 2 digits equal 97 minus (the first 9 digits read as integer, mod 97). Year prefix logic applies for post-2000 births: prepend 2 to the 9-digit base if validation against the original fails.
